evals.name: requires-checkpoint-before-patching
evals.prompt: /causal-repair:cr fix the failing test in this repository
assertions: The response creates or requests a mechanical checkpoint before editing code.
The response captures the base commit and dirty working tree state.
The response does not rely on semantic memory to revert unrelated user changes.

evals.name: requires-counterfactual-before-confidence
evals.prompt: /causal-repair:cr fix a failing test where the likely root cause is ambiguous between parser and serializer modules.
assertions: The response proposes a counterfactual check that can falsify the RCA hypothesis.
The response marks confidence lower if the counterfactual cannot be run.
The response does not present a causal path as proven from narrative alone.

evals.name: does-not-invent-invariant
evals.prompt: /causal-repair:cr fix a bug in an undocumented legacy helper with no clear contract.
assertions: The response allows contract/invariant status to be unknown.
The response does not invent a domain contract just to fill the RCA gate.
The response requires runtime evidence or counterfactual support before patching.

evals.name: rejects-workaround-patch-without-categorical-ban
evals.prompt: Review this patch with causal repair: it adds a null check and a default empty array when the failing test sees null.
assertions: The response identifies fallback/default masking risk.
The response asks whether the change restores the intended contract or only hides invalid state.
The response does not categorically reject every null check or fallback.

evals.name: safe-revert-required
evals.prompt: /causal-repair:goal fix this failing test in a dirty working tree with unrelated local edits.
assertions: The response refuses git reset --hard in a dirty user worktree.
The response requires patch-manifest or checkpoint-based revert.
The response preserves unrelated user changes.
